On January 3rd at 430pm, Dr. Vikram Mansharamani spoke with Dr. Mark Green about the unfolding situation in Venezuela. Dr. Green is a West Point graduate who served in the US Army, started and sold a successful medical company, and went on to serve in the Tennessee legislature before getting elected to the US House of Representatives, where he served as...

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Trump’s invasion of Venezuela is about oil, greed, and power.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Yes, Nicolas Maduro is an illegitimate dictator. That fact alone does not give Trump the authority to invade Venezuela.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260103-5779-footer&t=1&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)
Peter,
Let’s be clear about what happened overnight: Donald Trump ordered an illegal and unconstitutional attack on Venezuela, with no authorization from Congress, and no plan for what comes next.
That’s why I didn’t hesitate to go on MSNOW and lay out exactly what’s at stake in this moment:
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260103-2.ROGvE7&refcode=em20260103-5779&t=2&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)
This is how forever wars start. It’s not about drug trafficking — if it were, Trump wouldn’t have pardoned the former Honduran president who was convicted for his role in a drug trafficking scheme that smuggled 400 tons of cocaine into the United States.
**This is about oil, greed, and power. And it’s about a president who lied when he said he’d put a stop to endless wars and focus on making life affordable for people here at home.**
Trump can’t even run America, and now he wants to run Venezuela.
Yes, Nicolas Maduro is an illegitimate dictator. That fact alone does not give Trump the authority to invade another country, kidnap him in the middle of the night, and force regime change. We’ve tried that playbook before, and it has failed over and over again. And it costs working people the most.
Congress, not the president, has the sole power to authorize war. Republicans need to step up, do their Constitutional duty, and join Democrats in ensuring that Congress reasserts our authority and puts a stop to this.
Because if we allow Trump to get away with this, we’re setting a precedent where any rogue leader can push regime change anywhere they want across the world.
I’m fighting back, but I can’t do it alone. I don’t take corporate PAC money, and I don’t answer to oil executives or defense contractors. I answer to the working people who have the most to lose if we start yet another endless war.
[**That’s why I’m asking you to chip in $3 or whatever you can right now to help me keep up the fight to stop forever wars and rein in Trump’s abuse of power.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260103-2.ROGvE7&refcode=em20260103-5779&amount=3&t=3&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260103-2.ROGvE7&amount=3&refcode=em20260103-5779&t=22&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)**
The American people have said over and over again that we do not want another war. If we’re going to stop this one, we have to step up together.
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20260103-5779-footer&t=23&refcode2=5779_420207_Y4eQw3&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=24&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=25&akid=5779%2E420207%2EY4eQw3)

Paid for by Pramila for Congress

name: Kyle Blomquist for Congress
subject: “We’re going to run thecountry”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blomquist for Congress
body:
I condemn the U.S. government's illegal invasion of Venezuela | | | We’ve seen this before Peter, I condemn the U.S. government's illegal invasion of Venezuela in the strongest possible terms. The actions of the Trump administration are war crimes and must be denounced as such. This was not about freedom for the people of Venezuela. After all, this is the same man who said this: It was not about fentanyl. Data from Trump’s own DEA shows virtually no illicit fentanyl comes from Venezuela. It was not about drug trafficking. Trump just freed and pardoned former Honduran president Juan Orlando Hernández, who was convicted of conspiring to smuggle 400 tons of cocaine. **I remember the beginning of the Iraq War.** After that administration quickly declared “mission accomplished,” we endured years of chaos, hundreds of thousands of lives lost in Iraq, 4,431 American troops killed, an even more destabilized region that led to the rise of ISIS, and an estimated $2.9 trillion in costs as of 2025. That money could have gone to schools, health care, and infrastructure. Instead, it enriched the military-industrial complex and their executives. **Northern Michigan has seen what happens when leaders ignore the lessons of the past.** We deserve representation that remembers how these stories actually end and refuses to repeat them. Peter, we must hold this administration accountable in 2026.

clean_body: I condemn the U.S. government's illegal invasion of Venezuela | | | We’ve seen this before Peter, I condemn the U.S. government's illegal invasion of Venezuela in the strongest possible terms. The actions of the Trump administration are war crimes and must be denounced as such. This was not about freedom for the people of Venezuela. After all, this is the same man who said this: It was not about fentanyl. Data from Trump’s own DEA shows virtually no illicit fentanyl comes from Venezuela. It was not about drug trafficking. Trump just freed and pardoned former Honduran president Juan Orlando Hernández, who was convicted of conspiring to smuggle 400 tons of cocaine. I remember the beginning of the Iraq War. After that administration quickly declared “mission accomplished,” we endured years of chaos, hundreds of thousands of lives lost in Iraq, 4,431 American troops killed, an even more destabilized region that led to the rise of ISIS, and an estimated $2.9 trillion in costs as of 2025. That money could have gone to schools, health care, and infrastructure. Instead, it enriched the military-industrial complex and their executives. Northern Michigan has seen what happens when leaders ignore the lessons of the past. We deserve representation that remembers how these stories actually end and refuses to repeat them. Peter, we must hold this administration accountable in 2026. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Or mail a check to: P.O. Box 66, Iron Mountain, MI 49801 – Kyle Paid for by Blomquist for Congress P.O. Box 66, Iron Mountain, MI 49801 Kyle Blomquist is running to represent the people of Michigan’s 1st District in the U.S. House of Representatives, on a mission to unify working people and dramatically improve their lives. | | Sent via . name: Rose Penelope Yee
subject: Condemning the Lawless Seizure of Venezuelan President
disclaimer_text: Paid for by [Rose for Congress](https://roseforcongress
body:
Democrats must speak with courage and urgency. Congress must reclaim its war powers authority and call for Trump’s impeachment.

| | | | | [|
---](https://roseforcongress.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=35bf91c695e8f4a5dc8a9dd42&id=d2ce8ce926&e=8c53948e4c)
| | | | | |

# **Diplomacy Instead of Lawlessness**
---
| | Dear friends,

I believe in America’s capacity to lead through compassion, diplomacy, and the rule of law.
The reported attacks on civilians, raids on boats and murder of its occupants, continued harsh economic sanctions, and now the capture of Venezuela’s president and his wife represent a dangerous escalation that undermines international law and further erodes our moral standing in the world.Regime-change wars America has led - from Iraq to Libya to Afghanistan - have cost working people trillions of dollars, destabilized entire regions, and produced lasting humanitarian crises. Wars do not create democracy.Let us be clear: this is not about freedom or human rights. It is about power, resources, and corporate interests - especially Venezuela's oil reserves. The American people are tired of being misled into endless conflict.Democrats must speak with courage and urgency. **Congress must reclaim its war powers authority and call for Trump’s impeachment**. We must reject further military escalation against Venezuela and demand an immediate ceasefire, the return of U.S. forces and equipment, the lifting of sanctions that punish civilians, and a renewed commitment to diplomacy.There is still a peaceful path forward. By choosing de-escalation and international cooperation, we can once again speak with integrity about justice, sovereignty, and the rule of law. Let’s redirect our resources toward the well-being of people here at home.I believe that by choosing a path of diplomacy, we uphold Congress’ constitutional authority and abide with international law.
When I am elected as your next member of Congress, I will work to end reckless foreign entanglements and replace them with diplomatic cooperation that actually keeps all people safe.
Yours in upholding diplomacy and the rule of law,
**Rose Penelope Yee

PS. **We are gaining momentum as we push our campaign forward in January. Your contribution is very much appreciated.
---

_**EXHIBIT 1 - JOINT FUNDRAISING NOTICE **_

Team Nunn ("JFC") is a joint fundraising committee composed of Iowans for Zach Nunn ("Campaign"), Call to Service PAC ("LPAC") and NRCC (each, a "Committee," and, collectively, the "Committees"). Any contribution to the JFC permissible under the Federal Election Campaign Act, as amended ("FECA"), from contributors who have not exceeded their applicable federal contribution limits, shall be allocated among the Committees pursuant to FECA and the formula set forth in this Notice.

_**Contributions Made by an Individual or Federal Non-Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Individuals or federal non-multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $322,100. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $7,000, designating up to $3,500 to the primary election and up to $3,500 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3\. NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $44,300 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.
5\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $132,900 for its Legal Proceedings Fund, provided such limits apply.

_**Contributions Made by a Federal Multicandidate Political Committee:**_
Multicandidate PACs may contribute up to $120,000. Such contributions shall be allocated in the following order:
1\. Campaign shall receive any contribution not exceeding $10,000, designating up to $5,000 to the primary election and up to $5,000 to the general election, provided such limits apply.
2\. LPAC shall receive any contribution not exceeding $5,000 annually.
3 .NRCC shall receive for its general account any contribution not exceeding $15,000 annually.
4\. NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $45,000 for its Building Fund, provided such limits apply.
5 .NRCC shall receive any contribution not to exceed $45,000 for its Legal Proceedings Fund, provided such limits apply.

Contributions to Team Nunn, or to any participating Committee individually, are not deductible as charitable donations for federal income tax purposes. Contributions from corporations, labor organizations, national banks, federal government contractors, foreign nationals without green cards, and other federally impermissible sources are prohibited. The contributor's signature designates his or her contribution to the elections described above. Contributions that exceed a donor's contribution limit to a participating Committee shall be reallocated to the remaining Committees according to this allocation formula to the extent permitted by FECA. Any contributor may designate his or her contribution to a specific participant Committee to the extent permitted by FECA. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer of each individual whose aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle or calendar year, as applicable.
Paid for by Team Nunn, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Iowans for Zach Nunn, Call to Service PAC, and NRCC.

name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: My trial begins Monday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust
body:
There comes a point in every man’s life...
| Dear Peter,
---
There comes a point in every man’s life when you wonder how history will remember you.
---
No matter what happens in my RIGGED trial _this _Monday__ , I know with absolute confidence that history will say: **_“Rudy W. Giuliani was a fighter.”_**
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/7BCvA4x6cd5qYvLOdPE2GYWLDim1bTOmFepUnpLFeUDjZcLzGrgOXPbX_DKNoCythKntuOPB3KgepEfYEJaJJXUIyBzFsOm279z4vb6qj6u3hmXLinhDwiybQgD78AeR0N36FDufu6GK1_vspuIrUjQR1ybgXa1ZUXIqiBOPkOW11L97AtVzdX-erEogyFWUr5ZHHaG4_Xf1VMXSe9cX1brOYV0soqslgz-mXTUlfbkHRZeomvHyrPtoLtEDm8an5j655G9w9q42M_N-kTUBes7RDNvQEeEkbxw4bjtybJ5izRx6IC-g7STBSHuFA7NpRB83fJu3glSCc4L37iJUJbkrnAtfeb421K-a0fRki52TbUoa4NmWRKbnUFPjG5s33cb2Eh4qjiU4doqYUD0f_Gz-C1MfmqeaTH4y95PO7A3nvVucB4rkFJYtFacGvDL96rJJE9eHtzV_FirKtbsCdKrfoE2W0snY7Cnu_tZoydUm5roUHicQNoOCPBf6e5YxWFua3JLZIgG9RJypsjDlCC4LYqLC74_V-iw-io4YgrjML3oeN0Cvq9CUZjTO1Bek3iDelBOtRe2L1kBm74LuzlvFARdgNAY8azyv6_JP7kEQxNry7fdezU-kH5nMNdTjARrwO-d4AK40QLzu7fecOlmpDZeKJ37618i8lvivnBJ8b_kMcb7J0VEP28oCVjSMw039If9y_avF6qn4b9xnevnyO9itaMuamVYcyK0OXmXKtefcqIcEreztJEz7jxh6_ZeZn8M6TlOXJqM82lAzdJSSl572ehDc8nj4cLWgpVBv/4oGEB1b8tHBeqDwr1V_5AbKyEs3D6tYa/16)
__
---
**I fought the Mafia, I fought violent criminals, I fought corruption, I fought the Deep State agents who tried to frame and destroy my client – President Trump.**
---
_Now, at 81 years old, rather than fighting for someone else’s freedom and justice, I find myself fighting for my** _own_**._
---
**That’s why I officially filed a NEW _Defense_ _Trust_ in the conservative-friendly state of Florida that will give me _more power_ to fight the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for LIFE – with my trial beginning on _January 5_.**
---
_Peter, I am going to be brutally honest with you: right now, I lack the necessary means to fully fund my Defense Trust. Trust me, admitting that is not my strong suit._
---
But to beat the bloodthirsty vengeful Democrats, I’m going to need every cent I can get.
---
_I’m going up against the machine that let James Comey and Letitia James off the hook – the machine that had me and the President ARRESTED in the most violent jail in America._
---
With your help, history will forever remember that we took on the machine – and _WON_!
---
_But, if you can’t give right now (I know we’re in the midst of the holidays, and you likely have a lot on your plate and your mind), then please just carry on with your day._

clean_body: There comes a point in every man’s life... | Dear Peter, There comes a point in every man’s life when you wonder how history will remember you. No matter what happens in my RIGGED trial this Monday , I know with absolute confidence that history will say: “Rudy W. Giuliani was a fighter.” I fought the Mafia, I fought violent criminals, I fought corruption, I fought the Deep State agents who tried to frame and destroy my client – President Trump. Now, at 81 years old, rather than fighting for someone else’s freedom and justice, I find myself fighting for my own. That’s why I officially filed a NEW Defense Trust in the conservative-friendly state of Florida that will give me more power to fight the Left’s FINAL attempt to imprison me for LIFE – with my trial beginning on January 5. Peter, I am going to be brutally honest with you: right now, I lack the necessary means to fully fund my Defense Trust. Trust me, admitting that is not my strong suit. But to beat the bloodthirsty vengeful Democrats, I’m going to need every cent I can get. I’m going up against the machine that let James Comey and Letitia James off the hook – the machine that had me and the President ARRESTED in the most violent jail in America. With your help, history will forever remember that we took on the machine – and WON! But, if you can’t give right now (I know we’re in the midst of the holidays, and you likely have a lot on your plate and your mind), then please just carry on with your day. Thank you and God Bless you! Rudy Giuliani Rudy Defense Trust Paid for by Rudy Defense Trust |

Federal prosecutors have laid out a sweeping criminal case accusing Nicolas Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores de Maduro, of leading a narco-state operation that pumped thousands of tons of cocaine into the United States over more than two decades.

According to the superseding indictment, Maduro sat “at the forefront” of a corrupt network that abused Venezuela’s government institutions to move massive quantities of cocaine north, enrich regime insiders, and protect violent drug traffickers and narco-terrorist groups.

Prosecutors say the scheme dates back to the late 1990s and continued through Maduro’s rise from legislator to foreign minister to president and now de facto ruler.
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2026/01/u-s-indictment-accuses-venezuelan-dictator-nicolas-maduro-and-wife-of-running-decades-long-cocaine-empire-targeting-america.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=282ee781a8-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
Nicolas Maduro arrives at Stewart Air National Guard Base in New York. (FaytuksNetwork/X)
The indictment describes a pattern of corruption that followed Maduro through every office he held. As a member of Venezuela’s National Assembly, he allegedly moved cocaine under the protection of law enforcement.

As foreign minister, prosecutors say he sold Venezuelan diplomatic passports to known traffickers and used diplomatic cover to shield private planes ferrying drug money from Mexico back to Venezuela.

As president, the indictment alleges, Maduro allowed cocaine-fueled corruption to flourish for his own benefit, the benefit of his ruling clique, and the benefit of his family.

At the center of the case is what prosecutors describe as an illegitimate government that weaponized state power to protect and promote drug trafficking. The indictment names senior regime figures, including Diosdado Cabello Rondón and Ramón Rodríguez Chacín, as co-conspirators who enriched themselves and entrenched their political power through cocaine profits.

Prosecutors allege Maduro and his allies partnered with some of the most violent criminal organizations in the world, including Colombia’s FARC and ELN, Mexico’s Sinaloa Cartel and Los Zetas, and Venezuela’s own Tren de Aragua.

These alliances allegedly ensured cocaine production, protection, and transport from Colombia through Venezuela, across the Caribbean and Central America, and ultimately into the United States.

The indictment details a sophisticated logistics network. By 2020, the U.S. State Department estimated that 200 to 250 tons of cocaine moved through Venezuela each year. Shipments traveled by go-fast boats, fishing vessels, container ships, and aircraft departing from clandestine dirt airstrips and even commercial airports controlled by corrupt officials. Along the route, traffickers paid off politicians and security forces in transshipment countries such as Honduras, Guatemala, and Mexico to guarantee safe passage.

The case also places Maduro’s family at the heart of the operation. Prosecutors allege that Flores de Maduro accepted bribes to broker protection for traffickers and that other family members, including Maduro’s son, benefited from the cocaine trade.

name: AMAC Action
subject: American elections should be decided by American citizens
body:
It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math.

| |
---
| | It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math.
---
| |
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zv6b7rf469kioqs0r9j7kenarkt0k9bcasqe02sg_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)
---
| | **“****A disproportionate number of illegal immigrants go to blue states, they amplify the effect of a blue state vote. My understanding is that Democrats would lose approximately 20 seats in the house if illegals were not counted in census. And that’s also 20 less electoral votes for president.**** ** **So, illegals absolutely do impact who controls the House and the presidency.”****~** Elon Musk
---
| | Fellow Conservative,Elon Musk is right. It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. It’s also a ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris – in the form of 11.5 million illegals entering our country on their watch – one which will explode shortly after President Trump leaves office in 2029.While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. The truth is, without immediate action, these illegal aliens will be counted in the U.S. Census, which determines both the apportionment of seats in the House of Representatives _and_ the number of electoral votes given to each state. Whether they came because, four years ago, Kamala Harris and Joe Biden prompted them to “surge to the border” for asylum or because of blue-states offering sanctuary status, “free” health care, “free” cell phones, “free” housing, and “free” college...it doesn’t matter. The result is the same.Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in Congress. Your vote is _already_ being **watered down** in the Electoral College.And every day reports are popping up of non-citizens, illegal aliens, and convicted felons being registered to vote in cities and entire swing-states across the country, which means your vote may be fully **canceled out in future elections**. **_American elections should be decided by American citizens._****[If you agree then please sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution so the chaos we’re seeing now _never_ happens again in America.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z1tpv67i6vhsm1cp109rs43jgvqu0sb0qrqcrquo_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)**Because, here’s the scary part:The last census took place in 2020. That was _before_ the 11.5 million illegal aliens who invaded our country under Joe Biden and Kamala Harris. To put it in context, there are only 8 states in America with a population over 11 million. So, it’s as if the radical Left just imported the 9th largest state in America. That’s more than North Carolina (10.8 million), Michigan (10 million), New Jersey (9.2 million), or Virginia (8.7 million).You get the picture. That’s why urgent action is needed to pass a Constitutional Amendment to ban illegal aliens from voting and from being counted for purposes of congressional and electoral vote apportionment. The next census in less than 6 years away. If those 11.5 million illegal aliens are counted in the census or are allowed to vote in the intervening years, you and I simply won’t have a country. **[It’s vitally important that your representatives in Washington and in your state legislature hear this demand directly from you.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zr4vtnse8p3qgq3p01mnksdgkhp5vara7gpi122o_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)** Because, quite honestly, they think they can pull a fast one on you. They think you and I will be so relieved to see the border secure again and criminal illegal aliens deported, that you and I will be oblivious to this threat lying dormant under our very eyes.No way.My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. **AMAC Action is the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.****We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices.** **Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of our 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.****And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the** **U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the issues.** Quite simply, when we speak, Washington listens. [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zomi6r4r9u981ujeb2n3uksor8fsmki8ocbnro20_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)**[And AMAC Action needs your help today in this do-or-die battle to pass a Constitutional Amendment to protect your vote from being canceled out by an illegal alien or a convicted felon.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z5crfoghijtj9m2b2a37g0j8c692d4islpidt3ug_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)** But I can’t do it without you. **No matter what issue you care about the most, if you and I don’t have a secure,_honest_ elections... none of it matters.**Inflation. Illegal immigration. Violent Crime. Drug use. Homelessness. The 2nd Amendment. Out of control spending. Social Security and Medicare insolvency. Right to Life. Indoctrination in our schools. Endless foreign wars. You name it. **[So please follow this link to sign your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z98f496v5kh1smcvvf75bdqf65rv3k2bda1ijlm8_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)** so that AMAC Action can put your representatives in Washington and in the state legislature on notice with a simple demand: Namely, that you will not tolerate this erosion of your vote.Now, here’s the bad news:Most state constitutions grant voting rights to “every” citizen, open-ended language that liberal activists argue permits localities to authorize non-citizen and even illegal alien voting. States should amend their constitutions to instead permit “only” citizens to vote. But there’s good news, too:Seven states have _expressly_ banned non-citizen voting already: Alabama, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Louisiana, North Dakota, and Ohio.**What’s more, voters approve of constitutional amendments to ban non-citizen voting by overwhelming margins. Nearly 77% of Ohio voters approved a measure in 2022, while nearly 63% of Colorado voters approved one in 2020.** This is a solution whose time has come. You and I can do this. **[That’s why, when you’ve signed your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zkab5eqia53rokj9ftb55plhv6c3pvt1k14d2bq8_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs), I'm also asking you to make a one-time contribution of $35 to give AMAC Action the resources to send out, collect, process, and tabulate millions of petitions.** The reality is, we don’t get a dime of taxpayer funding. Our funding comes from grassroots conservatives like you who care deeply about the future of America. This is important. _It means we can’t be bought._ On the other hand, the leftwing election fraud machine is funded by leftwing billionaires like Mark Zuckerberg and George Soros who are actively funneling _billions_ to dark money “non-profit” groups to “**reshape** ” our elections and our electorate.With that said, if you’re able to make a larger contribution – say $50, $75, $125, $250, $500, or even $1,000 – it would really be a life saver. Please hurry. In order to secure your vote in all future elections – and with 11.5 million illegals chomping to be included in the next census – **[your “BAN NON-CITIZEN VOTING” Petition to Amend the U.S. Constitution is vitally important.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z543fcv1mdjjjv73it797f8tk84aaf16oenfot9g_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clh62ob1cth2bs)** Yes, this is about saving your vote from being canceled. It’s also about saving America.And it all starts with the straightforward premise that non-citizens and illegal aliens – no matter how many Kamala Harris and Joe Biden imported on their watch – should be banned from voting _and_ banned from being counted for apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes. Thank you in advance for joining the fight to secure your vote

clean_body: It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. | | | | It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. | | | | “A disproportionate number of illegal immigrants go to blue states, they amplify the effect of a blue state vote. My understanding is that Democrats would lose approximately 20 seats in the house if illegals were not counted in census. And that’s also 20 less electoral votes for president. So, illegals absolutely do impact who controls the House and the presidency.”~ Elon Musk | | Fellow Conservative,Elon Musk is right. It isn’t rocket-science. It’s basic math. It’s also a ticking time-bomb set by Joe Biden and Kamala Harris – in the form of 11.5 million illegals entering our country on their watch – one which will explode shortly after President Trump leaves office in 2029.While you and I have no doubt President Trump will deliver on his promise to secure the border, this threat will not go away. The truth is, without immediate action, these illegal aliens will be counted in the U.S. Census, which determines both the apportionment of seats in the House of Representatives and the number of electoral votes given to each state. Whether they came because, four years ago, Kamala Harris and Joe Biden prompted them to “surge to the border” for asylum or because of blue-states offering sanctuary status, “free” health care, “free” cell phones, “free” housing, and “free” college...it doesn’t matter. The result is the same.Your vote is already being watered down in Congress. Your vote is already being watered down in the Electoral College.And every day reports are popping up of non-citizens, illegal aliens, and convicted felons being registered to vote in cities and entire swing-states across the country, which means your vote may be fully canceled out in future elections. American elections should be decided by American citizens., here’s the scary part:The last census took place in 2020. That was before the 11.5 million illegal aliens who invaded our country under Joe Biden and Kamala Harris. To put it in context, there are only 8 states in America with a population over 11 million. So, it’s as if the radical Left just imported the 9th largest state in America. That’s more than North Carolina (10.8 million), Michigan (10 million), New Jersey (9.2 million), or Virginia (8.7 million).You get the picture. That’s why urgent action is needed to pass a Constitutional Amendment to ban illegal aliens from voting and from being counted for purposes of congressional and electoral vote apportionment. The next census in less than 6 years away. If those 11.5 million illegal aliens are counted in the census or are allowed to vote in the intervening years, you and I simply won’t have a country. Because, quite honestly, they think they can pull a fast one on you. They think you and I will be so relieved to see the border secure again and criminal illegal aliens deported, that you and I will be oblivious to this threat lying dormant under our very eyes.No way.My name is Rebecca Weber. I’m the chairwoman of AMAC Action. AMAC Action is the most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative Seniors.We’ve got 72 chapters and 300 volunteer delegates, who are the “feet on the street” advocates who contact Members of Congress and state legislators in their district offices. Moreover, our chapters and delegates help organize Town Hall meetings with elected officials and candidates to enable those locally of our 2,000,000 AMAC members to meet, hear, and question them.And you should also know that we now have more than 337,000 AMAC members who respond to our “calls-to-action” and weigh in with members of the U.S. House and Senate, as well as their state legislators on the issues. Quite simply, when we speak, Washington listens. But I can’t do it without you. No matter what issue you care about the most, if you and I don’t have a secure,honest elections... none of it matters.Inflation. Illegal immigration. Violent Crime. Drug use. Homelessness. The 2nd Amendment. Out of control spending. Social Security and Medicare insolvency. Right to Life. Indoctrination in our schools. Endless foreign wars. You name it. so that AMAC Action can put your representatives in Washington and in the state legislature on notice with a simple demand: Namely, that you will not tolerate this erosion of your vote.Now, here’s the bad news:Most state constitutions grant voting rights to “every” citizen, open-ended language that liberal activists argue permits localities to authorize non-citizen and even illegal alien voting. States should amend their constitutions to instead permit “only” citizens to vote. But there’s good news, too:Seven states have expressly banned non-citizen voting already: Alabama, Arizona, Colorado, Florida, Louisiana, North Dakota, and Ohio.What’s more, voters approve of constitutional amendments to ban non-citizen voting by overwhelming margins. Nearly 77% of Ohio voters approved a measure in 2022, while nearly 63% of Colorado voters approved one in 2020. This is a solution whose time has come. You and I can do this. , I'm also asking you to make a one-time contribution of $35 to give AMAC Action the resources to send out, collect, process, and tabulate millions of petitions. The reality is, we don’t get a dime of taxpayer funding. Our funding comes from grassroots conservatives like you who care deeply about the future of America. This is important. It means we can’t be bought. On the other hand, the leftwing election fraud machine is funded by leftwing billionaires like Mark Zuckerberg and George Soros who are actively funneling billions to dark money “non-profit” groups to “reshape ” our elections and our electorate.With that said, if you’re able to make a larger contribution – say $50, $75, $125, $250, $500, or even $1,000 – it would really be a life saver. Please hurry. In order to secure your vote in all future elections – and with 11.5 million illegals chomping to be included in the next census – Yes, this is about saving your vote from being canceled. It’s also about saving America.And it all starts with the straightforward premise that non-citizens and illegal aliens – no matter how many Kamala Harris and Joe Biden imported on their watch – should be banned from voting and banned from being counted for apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes. Thank you in advance for joining the fight to secure your vote | | For Freedom, Rebecca Weber,ChairwomanAMAC Action | | | P.S. American elections should be decided by American citizens. name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: The book Charlie left behind
body:
Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zanak33prtaqqqai0cc78jqv0ascii5tkv5chojg_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clj68or4c3h2bs)
---
| | Friend,
---
| | Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal.

It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war.

His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before...

**It was about the Sabbath. **

About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God.
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0f7ods2b1vheicrg97la1rkfjkq2s5kc005vnjo_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clj68or4c3h2bs)
---
**[And when you make a gift of any amount to Turning Point USA today, we'll send you a copy of Charlie's final book -- as our thank you for carrying his legacy forward.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zr3c3g8e2varjsrr4o22rflv8fi148t8kqf1n5fg_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clj68or4c3h2bs)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie's Final Book**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zbrvn999821163cmno18ea7vkepcnujg3nj32kfo_rp22sh2s8i6eor7clj68or4c3h2bs)
---
Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself.

He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling...

_**The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. **_

To rest. To reflect. To return to God.

This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation.

🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops
🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength
🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America

I know he would've wanted you to have this book.

**When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. **

clean_body: Charlie Kirk lived fast. And he lived loud. | | | | | Friend, | | Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal. It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war. His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before... It was about the Sabbath. About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God. Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself. He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling... The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. To rest. To reflect. To return to God. This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation. 🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops 🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength 🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America I know he would've wanted you to have this book. When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. Friend, thank you, in advance, for helping reach the next generation with truth, faith, and moral courage -- the very things Charlie gave his life for. And as you do, may the message of this book lead you -- just as it led Charlie -- to a deeper trust in God, a stronger love for your family, and a renewed peace in your own soul. For Charlie, Justin Streiff Chief Operating Officer Turning Point USA P.S. # IN YOUR WEEKLY BULLETIN:
---
|
---
| |
When public trust cracks, the truth rushes in. This week’s Sunday Bulletin dives headfirst into school board turmoil, taxpayer showdowns, and community flashpoints — from helicopter tragedy to “phone-free” classrooms, hospital oversight fights, and political lawfare in the courts. It’s accountability, regional heartbeat, high-stakes policy, and editorials that refuse to whisper when shouting is warranted.:
**INVESTIGATIVE** : A warning shot over Harrisburg’s handling of hospital closures as critics argue HB 1460 would tighten the grip of large healthcare systems, concentrate power, and worsen the very crisis it claims to solve.
**REGIONAL NEWS** : An Upper Salford elementary school burglary, a fatal Hammonton helicopter crash, and a $1M commitment to fight homelessness — alongside Ocean City’s push to lock down cell phone use in schools. Real communities, real stakes, no sugarcoating.
**POLITICS** : A courtroom clash over digital branding rights reveals a new era of political “lawfare,” where domain names become territory and litigation becomes a weapon.
**EDITORIAL** : From nuclear energy urgency to one-party school boards and a 340B program critics say is being gamed at patients’ expense — our op-eds pull the fire alarm on complacency.
---

name: The Flyover
subject: Maduro Deposed, Flossie Turns 30, and the Pentagon Pizza Theory
body:
President Trump said on Saturday the [U.S. will “run” Venezuela](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD3OnDAURVcDnUf-w4bCRZrpvrQpkbEfw0uMjezHoMnqI5IlpD7S1T3nx9f85TFhfn33Ozhay5zhagmIoM67j2ctEY7SIK6ptIZAZ81tllwaxgXjeo4e02fWfXQrgATVgxPW2EHqUUz95gRXdhzCooQxi9FyGUfwXAe7WCsnHXp09xoXXAvBzTA-pqgGq5RZ5KTtOqlOc4hIpT5-Fsy0wZo-5Q31EcreJ7cRHa1T3zr57OTzuq5HXsJtcfNOPo-SkDC0G25IwLZyNujkk-q5H-wNGX6fkDzLGEryjf3TZo0q_oLG6plZKGem-mFUfW5IWDLbMSH5-mE1ZC8HybXuq4vHhSlhM7bT_LV7TH9f0n-lfTv5JwAA__-IPZDi) after U.S. personnel captured President Nicolás Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, and carried out a “large-scale” strike inside the nation overnight.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuK3DAUhp9G7jzoZl0KFYFluk2bUsg6R2slsmUk7S7z9mHMQtrAlj985zu3X6_-NeSSj7efYUc3UvUHfvaCY2Dze4D3VgHP2hFSqb1nHO_t6J5TrmbKZio9hFweXk7gEiJHMaFjWumFS8PstLnFWGGFNgtbo0FpUXO6aC2YThJ40lN2TxtlVDJG1WJuFsSihVArt1InK4ikCHnUdvtd8zE2TOVRP7DdYt2n4rYxzk7ED8LvhN9HC_HPbcNQxva4-JDhSRJ-N0qsuOplVoaGWZqwzCtTacYoTNQADKwm4n6WEHHHYxDx8tXK97MevTb_lf8d6fDPybkiXAXIQMRL3EJuhKtxJcgN41j9f4v8Ve83NjUH52cuJXeliaRve8jl2nl861Efjv8NAAD__-_IqUQ) | |
# Sunday, January 4, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1896, after seven petitions to Congress, [Utah became the 45th state](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uwyAURp8Gb7bg8mcPDF28pWtHi5hLTIVNBDeO8vZV8gpdvuWTzpHOz2W5-JTTcfv2OzqKZTnw2TISYV12Hx61BLyXhiHm0lpCetSjLcDB9Fz0XC3Bp_xaVBdcRASUHTphjdWgRjF1m5twVcqrSfurUdHqVYYgr6MVRsdxHNcuuTeNC66E4EaPwxSktlKaK0zKxkkyxTEkKnX4LemgDWN-lRPrsJa9y24jujcmvxjMDGZf1y2d2IYH-W24lZPBDBwEg5l_RjGY31_ra_Ghp9I38oRbKaEXoPsX-toYzF114f5MOadmLFP8tvuUP0r6V6fTwV8AAAD__9CNekM) when President Grover Cleveland signed a proclamation admitting it to the Union. Once synonymous with capturing life’s most important moments, Kodak taught generations how to remember. But as photography went digital and the business of memories changed, the company struggled to stay in the picture. In today’s _Whatever Happened To..._ , we look at how Kodak defined an era—and still managed to miss the future. Today’s sponsor, [Arthrozene](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkbuK3DAUhp9G7jzoZl0KFYFluk2bUsg6R2slsmUk7S7z9mHMQtrAlj985zu3X6_-NeSSj7efYUc3UvUHfvaCY2Dze4D3VgHP2hFSqb1nHO_t6J5TrmbKZio9hFweXk7gEiJHMaFjWumFS8PstLnFWGGFNgtbo0FpUXO6aC2YThJ40lN2TxtlVDJG1WJuFsSihVArt1InK4ikCHnUdvtd8zE2TOVRP7DdYt2n4rYxzk7ED8LvhN9HC_HPbcNQxva4-JDhSRJ-N0qsuOplVoaGWZqwzCtTacYoTNQADKwm4n6WEHHHYxDx8tXK97MevTb_lf8d6fDPybkiXAXIQMRL3EJuhKtxJcgN41j9f4v8Ve83NjUH52cuJXeliaRve8jl2nl861Efjv8NAAD__-_IqUQ), is sharing new research that suggests joint stiffness isn’t just about aging—and how a simple daily habit may help restore comfort and mobility.
|

## US Deposes Maduro, Will Run Venezuela
President Trump said on Saturday the [U.S. will “run” Venezuela](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD3OnDAURVcDnUf-w4bCRZrpvrQpkbEfw0uMjezHoMnqI5IlpD7S1T3nx9f85TFhfn33Ozhay5zhagmIoM67j2ctEY7SIK6ptIZAZ81tllwaxgXjeo4e02fWfXQrgATVgxPW2EHqUUz95gRXdhzCooQxi9FyGUfwXAe7WCsnHXp09xoXXAvBzTA-pqgGq5RZ5KTtOqlOc4hIpT5-Fsy0wZo-5Q31EcreJ7cRHa1T3zr57OTzuq5HXsJtcfNOPo-SkDC0G25IwLZyNujkk-q5H-wNGX6fkDzLGEryjf3TZo0q_oLG6plZKGem-mFUfW5IWDLbMSH5-mE1ZC8HybXuq4vHhSlhM7bT_LV7TH9f0n-lfTv5JwAA__-IPZDi) after U.S. personnel captured President Nicolás Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, and carried out a “large-scale” strike inside the nation overnight. The [U.S. targeted four confirmed sites](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1u7CAQBeCnwR0r_oztguI27va2KS0Mw-4kGCzAu3KePnJeIc00I30653zcl7vFiOnx325gWshLgneN0BqUZbP-KNnDniv4EHOtCO0oqS6CCU0Zp0wt3mI8F9V5EwAEyA4MH_TQCzXyqXsavbIg1ai0EM6tq9NqBN2P3PdWTiOMHZpLY5wpzpnux9vkZT9IqVcxqSFMkigGHlsut8-MqT0hxDO_oNxc3rponq3tlch_RMxEzOncc23Xi4j5gomYGb-OJGJ-5xI9vSoSMSvqcgpYNvA0Zmcb5lQpJupssc5W-oIE3wdES5stD2jg6XrSo1KLpbaCX3AxXTF-f2OMWPVAFHtsFuNvtvanQV9G_AQAAP__5QmKIw) across Caracas, including two military bases, a naval port, and an airport. Maduro and Flores were taken aboard the _USS Iwo Jima_ and [will face charges](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO6yAURVeDO0fwwGAXFL9xl99OaeHwiBmBseA5UXY_ymQJUx_pXp3zdV2uLqa43_-7jJZCWXZ8toREWJfs_FmLx6M09CGV1iLSWfe2AAfdc9FztXgX02tRnbcBEVB2aIXRZgA1iqnb7MBBwOodn0Apocc1gNFSORnCbeXBd9G-17jgSgiuh_EyeTkYKfUKkzJhkkxx9JFKvXyXuNOGIb3KA-vlVnKX7EZ0NCb_MZgZzLThFlN6MwbzVjK-hRjMzue4x0bVUSw7g3nQho_S9FTPfPTlwA_pb-6gs2LrP_oM5q5afzxjSrFpwxS_Zxd_Hzr6U7KHhZ8AAAD__4DPgPA) in the U.S., including narco-terrorism and cocaine importation conspiracies, as well as weapons charges. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth described the overnight attack as part of "[a massive joint military and law enforcement raid](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2u4yAUhJ_G7ogOPwZTUGyTLttuaWFziFkdGwtwouzTr3zvI9xyNNJovu_PY3r4RGl__vYbuhbztOO7EraGZdp8OEsOeOSKIVKuNWE7y14nAUIz4AzUFHyiz6T64CKiQNmj40abQaiR2351chYhaKUUGD4qO6shzstoh2g0hJHzPrlrDTgozkEP480GORgp9SysMtHKTgGG1HK5_c1pbytG-uQXltuSt57c2tpRO_mrE_dO3P1xAVzVFUpLC2En7q2c28G-gdgLd_x3Inl2FKwp4N6SJ3Z48guymfK75bKsWJmx2vJBeFTjwAFARrTLHNCKKBcOoi8uHO9ElKo2nYLn5hN9_Wo_kvly4n8AAAD__0rdiG4)" that lasted less than 30 minutes.

## List: Most Popular Fast Food by State
Chick-fil-A is [the most popular fast-food restaurant](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAYAJ8GOwx8IkJBcY3dXnulQfnY5YJi4HPNvv1l7xX-epKZZP48lodPOR3P335HR7EsB94tIxHWZffhqiXgWRqGmEtrCemqR1tAgOZCcqGW4FP-LKoLLiICDh06OelpBGWk7V5OBWMl6nVcrV6NWcNojYFt21bQZlO6S-5rE1IoKYUeTW_DME7DoFewaop2YEpgSFRq_7ekg14Y86e8sfZb2bvsXkRnY8MvBjOD-b7vvoYvYjD7SmnLyGDeSyN-lvPKvvLoG_FYSuAVG_mr-oMaTwfHN9YPb-QJOQgYGcxddeG8U86p6Ykp8dx9yv_L9KNdbwf_AgAA___OpH-j) in most states, according to a study analyzing Google Maps reviews across all 50 states. The chicken-centric chain earned the top ranking in 28 states, despite being closed on Sundays. [In-N-Out Burger](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D2O6yAUQOHVQGcLLn9xQfEad3ntlIiE6_iOsLGAxMruR5ktTH2krzhf13CNlGl__I8b-r6UsOPZMvaONWwxPWtJeJSGacmlNcL-rHsLIMAOQg5ChxQpv4PmyS-IgIqjl846A_oiJ756p6W5oFPupgGERJVSNAjWYXTSOMHJfzQhhZZSWHMZp6SMU8reYNJumRTTAhP1UsfvQntfccnv8sI63svGs197PxpT_xjMDObzPMeaPonBHGune0YGcy_HcHvWB9bhvkbaBxBgGMy8-nSclDM165gWjy1S_oX7n268PPwEAAD__-z5cfU) was the top-ranking chain in 13 states, despite having locations in only nine states. Five Guys, known for its fresh burgers and large portions of piping-hot fries, ranked No. 1 in six states. Raising Cane’s, KFC, and Taco Bell each ranked No. 1 in one state.

**Whatever happened to Kodak?** Kodak’s story is one of those American business arcs that feels almost mythic: a company that didn’t just dominate a category, but helped invent the way the modern world remembers itself—and then fell. For decades, Kodak made photography easy and ordinary. Its genius wasn’t only the camera—it was the whole system behind it. You’d buy a simple Kodak camera, load it with Kodak film, and then hand the roll back to a store or lab that would turn your memories into prints. That emotional sentiment was so powerful that _Mad Men_ used this [memorable sales pitch for the Kodak Carousel](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LtqwzAYxfGnkbcY3WzFg4ZCMF3SoVA6Cl0-xSqyZXSJydsX9xW6ngO_4f99V3cdYtgeH3oFWX1SGxwlQq2Q1apdy8nBngo4H1MpAWrLW1EU0_GCyQVz5XSIL8U7Jz0ABdaBJGIUA-VXMnWLHDDRYnTWDm4y1hvDPVg_YSv01RpsuyBPDRPMCcHjcO0nxwbB2GjoxIWfGOIYXKgp9z8pbHUBH1_pCbm3ae2iXGrdC2JviM6Izsdx9K_UajNw_ueiq10Qm5-I3Ur7vH3Ne3lvpcvS7UeIMZRRII4fqw7xj6z_6vCU9DcAAP__TqdysQ) slide projector to end its first season. The Kodak business loop—cameras up front, film and processing forever—created a machine that powered family snapshots, school portraits, weddings, vacations, and news photography on a global scale. By the postwar boom, Kodak wasn’t just a brand; it was practically a verb. Here are some of [its early TV ads.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFu6yAUxvGnwVss4GBjDwxXusqWLlXV0YKcQ0yFjQU4bt6-cl-h6_dJv-H_eZtuNsSwPt7sQqb6NK10lEi1Up4Wi3tOSFsqhD6mUgLVPa9lklz2Fy4uXE1oQ3xNqkHjiSRBQ0boXndSDWJsZiMGAXZQhIOTTqACJzvNPbcI_ehGbII5NS64EoL33dCOCJ0G6J0clfYjMMUJQ025_UphrTP5-EpPyu09LU00c61bYfCPySuT1-M42lfa6-7o_M_F1vvM4Ppk8H99DO_dtwC9fTTZ4HaEGEPpNVP8sdgQf8n6pw5PI38CAAD__9v5cKA) Then the world changed, and it changed in a way that attacked Kodak’s profit center. The company actually saw the future early: in 1975, a Kodak engineer built a crude prototype of a digital camera. But digital photography didn’t behave like film. It didn’t need chemical coating plants, processing counters, or repeat purchases of film rolls. Cameras and photography shifted toward electronics, software, and, eventually, phones—industries and economics Kodak wasn’t built around. Kodak tried to pivot. It sold digital cameras, chased home printing, invested in imaging technology, and leaned on its patent portfolio. But the collapse of film demand was steep and unforgiving, and the old business—capital-heavy, optimized for enormous volume—was brutally hard to shrink without breaking. In January 2012, Kodak filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. It emerged in 2013 as a much smaller company, refocused on commercial printing and industrial imaging rather than the consumer photo empire that once defined it. Kodak still exists, and film has even enjoyed a boutique comeback. But the Kodak that once owned “the way we take pictures” became a cautionary tale: when the technology changes the business model, being first to see the future doesn’t guarantee you get to live in it. _Did you own a Kodak camera? Do you still have envelopes stored somewhere filled with Kodak-made family photos? Let us know your experience by replying to this email._

() ➤Pizza orders near the Pentagon surged during the Venezuela attack, fueling the so-called Pentagon Pizza Theory, which links spikes in late-night orders to major national security events. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤Waking Up to Pee at Night? It’s Not Normal. If nighttime bathroom trips are disrupting your sleep, you’re not alone—but it’s not something you have to accept. A new report reveals the real reason so many adults wake up to pee and a simple way to support 24-hour bladder control, fewer urges, and fewer leaks. See what’s behind it and how to fix it. | Whatever happened to Kodak? Kodak’s story is one of those American business arcs that feels almost mythic: a company that didn’t just dominate a category, but helped invent the way the modern world remembers itself—and then fell. For decades, Kodak made photography easy and ordinary. Its genius wasn’t only the camera—it was the whole system behind it. You’d buy a simple Kodak camera, load it with Kodak film, and then hand the roll back to a store or lab that would turn your memories into prints. That emotional sentiment was so powerful that Mad Men used this slide projector to end its first season. The Kodak business loop—cameras up front, film and processing forever—created a machine that powered family snapshots, school portraits, weddings, vacations, and news photography on a global scale. By the postwar boom, Kodak wasn’t just a brand; it was practically a verb. Here are some of Then the world changed, and it changed in a way that attacked Kodak’s profit center. The company actually saw the future early: in 1975, a Kodak engineer built a crude prototype of a digital camera. But digital photography didn’t behave like film. It didn’t need chemical coating plants, processing counters, or repeat purchases of film rolls. Cameras and photography shifted toward electronics, software, and, eventually, phones—industries and economics Kodak wasn’t built around. Kodak tried to pivot. It sold digital cameras, chased home printing, invested in imaging technology, and leaned on its patent portfolio. But the collapse of film demand was steep and unforgiving, and the old business—capital-heavy, optimized for enormous volume—was brutally hard to shrink without breaking. In January 2012, Kodak filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. It emerged in 2013 as a much smaller company, refocused on commercial printing and industrial imaging rather than the consumer photo empire that once defined it. Kodak still exists, and film has even enjoyed a boutique comeback. But the Kodak that once owned “the way we take pictures” became a cautionary tale: when the technology changes the business model, being first to see the future doesn’t guarantee you get to live in it. Did you own a Kodak camera? Do you still have envelopes stored somewhere filled with Kodak-made family photos? ## National Democrats Targeting Texas House
The Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee (DLCC) is spending $50 million nationwide [to flip 42 state legislative chambers](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGOpDAMhp8GuowSkwFSUFxDt9deiUxiIFpDUOJZdt_-xNwjXGfJ_j_rs_98TB8YOR7rb9xpkO8lTQddhUmE8rTgJ0VPuNIhmDMVoRCQGSfQ0CptlLZTwMg_k63DMOvgTE2D6druCb3u-3obnO1ssziv7RyeunXN0zgL82yctcZ3WMfhhmmjrQFtoH10s23AOdQGvO98U1lNIUrKD9lI6BvLwj_pi_LDp73mYRM5S9X8qmCsYLyu6_H59aK7WcGIWaJnqmC8vSoYz8RRor_LN0sxrbEwyiu_p1BiOpBVoD35jFJUppJe2VNR_wJbOqmoheN5xmNVRVBIbelVSBW6A-_j7DEI5V0Rk7-R90JoneodhdaCV63RjbILBOXQN2p2rn-Cc72luc5DOK_IHEvbVVavO0Z-28r__ehrgL8BAAD__xOXpgY), including significant first-time investments in Texas after several campaign cycles. Republicans have controlled the Texas House since 2003 and currently hold an 88-62 majority in the chamber. While the DLCC has not released an official list of races they are targeting, officials are [eyeing five districts](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OgzAQhU9jOiPb_BgKF9vQZdst0WAPYMVgZE9CcvsV2SNsNdI8faP53s9tvIEPfl--YUNDrzmOO545IBGmcYY7eouw4E6QEmZC5yAEGJVQLReSi3p04MN7rAtnJuF6WaCRutWN6kTXFatpNchJu8mqqmlU34iurmbsQbh-lnqCwpvrmJCilkpI1ZZ6qivV9yCkslbbitUCnaeYSlqR8AV5Du_4xFTauBXBrERHZtUXUwNTw3me5f0F-xUyNVw2TA0fjB8xePL2WrzjIyFfot8XTpGv8MRrzn5ZiT8y_wMcbtEmoMwJ0oLEMwEhX-MjI8_4CSKfgz-43_nlwdRQJOOO04fgc6tZLZYNfPj8Sv9r-GnUbwAAAP__ZvGSFg) in Corpus Christi, San Benito, Garland, and San Antonio. Democrat Beto O’Rourke, who unsuccessfully ran for the U.S. Senate in 2018, would have carried three of those districts had the current map been in place. One of them—House District 118 in San Antonio—[is an open seat](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGS5CAMRU-DM7pAZowdEGzibDbd0KUGYVODTRdo2tO333JfYRIlT1L99_99Lp-YcjrWv7iT459YloPOlomZ6hLxi5InXOlgrJUaUwiYMy6gYJBKS2WWgCm_FtMFd1dh0h05bQf7AaMax25z5q79FMYYIgXEGCbQsbc0mnEYJuttl9z1TGllNCgNw83eTQ_ThEqD99b3wigKiUu98UZMP9hifpUn1Zsve5fdxvxoov8jYBYwn-d5-2rIFxQwXzYC5veZgBkUfAiYNVxDXRw5lQOzDLQXX5GbfHyzfO_LrXw3kuWQOTWWJcpMa2oZOT1JMtaVuMlYqrwMBMxddeFxppxTG6wwat0x5XdK_l23Twf_AwAA___RcY3R) because Republican incumbent John Lujan is running for Congress. Newly appointed House Democratic Campaign Committee Chair Christina Morales said Texas has more Democratic voters than Republicans, and targeted investment and resources can energize them and boost turnout.
## Fake ICE Officer Arrested
Galveston police arrested a 44-year-old man accused of [impersonating an ICE officer](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu3CAQRb8Gd6xgYG1cUKRx99KmtMYw9qJgsGC0-_bvo42UL0h9patzzq-v9QtTTuX4iSd5_t7rWujVMzFTW3f8TSkQHlQYW6POFCPmjCsoGKXSUtk1Ysrv1Q7RbyrOeiCvp3G6g1PODQ8PiBNZF3e3q02FHTet57u5j3MwztzjkPznTGllNSgN423arIF5RqUhhCkYYRXFxLXd-EFM39j3_K5PardQzyH7B_PVhfkhYBGw4Ba0-SwClqt2FrA8U6QqOV0yE8YuTyzyn44MD2wHRZnOi1qvBTmVQ2Z8SSp7bYFOKiwPzE_qXIu8ouz47gIW7Yw1DkDAMjQfr1fKOfVxElYdJ6b8F4__L-rTw58AAAD__6ywjH4) after receiving a complaint from a witness who recorded his alleged attempt to detain two people. Joshua Warner is [charged](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0MGOpCAQxvGnwRsGClQ8cNiLt9nrHk0BhZKhZYJsO_32GzvZJ5hzJf_U7_vzsX5gyunYfuODbPuOZT3oOjO1RnWN-EnJE250NKyVzkYhYM64goCRC8mFXgOm_Fp1F6wTYZYdWTmN0wBGGNPtVgrlTBRxGEEJPZCP5I02TqHyephDl-wdE1JoCULC2E9OK5hnFBK8n7xiWlBIrdS-7dToG8-YX-VJtffl0WW7t_Z1MvWLwcJgua6r_9zL3_vIYMHaks_EYLldDJZcPGYGy4b5SWcrB38n-W3lyRMvMSZPlf8HM1jADNwr54SbDB8pjFwrNNx5rbgPcqIhODVg6KoNX1fKOZ3jxLTYHpjy-8v2s22fFv4FAAD__w-xjD4) with two counts of impersonating a public servant and is being held in the Galveston County Jail on a $500,000 bond. Investigators are asking others who may have been unlawfully detained by Warner to come forward. Galveston activists Robert Harvey and Bruce Rebmann approached Warner on Nov. 9, questioning his legitimacy, [with Harvey saying](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2yqzAMhVdjOjOyIPwULl5Dl9feklFsGTwxdgY7Idn9HbKF26iS9J3z_VznK_ng4_KfNtbl7dIc-ciBS-F9dnRnb5gWjoX2nXNhaykEmhGwk6AktLMlHz5zW1l9AzuqirXqu_6CAwxDtWrTjM2tARwcDooHq9wAcOmNGx0AX1Tl9fkMFLQKQWFX97e2wXEkUGhMbxrRAltf0l6XlQu_KbvwSS_ea5O2Kui1lEcWzT-Bk8DpOI7aBG_uuKZnLimeWwKns5bAKSRDQeB0MgVOoM7RnHe-RM6ZszQpuj3FIjeKkox5ZrYyOflI2cdFUpbesPxKkT7KhcKLT5DAqdq1fRw-BJ-7XrSwbOTDN2b5m9yXxt8AAAD__3SEjcs) the uniform looked cheaply made and suspicious. Warner initially refused to provide his name or badge number, then was arrested on Dec. 22 after authorities reported finding a fake badge, false ID, and a vehicle resembling an unmarked patrol car.
## Dallas Highways Shut Down During NYE Show
Traffic west of downtown Dallas ground to a halt on New Year’s Eve as drivers stopped to watch fireworks and a drone display at Reunion Tower. Watch a video [here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFvszAQxvFPY7ag82EMDB5e6VWkDunQpSM625dg1QmRuYTQT1_Rr9D1eaTf8P88jSdKOd0u73RlJ6_zPN54XTKLcBnP9MUpMF34JlQKL8IxUs40IqA9gD6AGSOlvI2mis5DHHTFTne2a7GHvq8m5w22g27RDtBba7XxRJ5NiBB0H7irktsx0GA0gkZbd940OAwEGkPoQqMMcEwyl1omFn7Rcs7b_ORSh_laZTeJ3BfV_FN4VHhc17Xe5oc8PO__vpCESTXHp2r-v77v7duHPLYDVMXF-5pyTovtlIHLlVL-JeVvIZ4OfwIAAP__XwVxKQ). After leaving work around 11 p.m., Melissa Macatee got stuck in traffic on the Margaret Hunt Hill Bridge, where she saw people [celebrating](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOgzAQRL8GOiN7cYAULq6hy7VXogWvwcoGR7YvDn9_Ir9w3RSj0Zv3c5tu6Nnv6zc-yOS3C9NOJTHlTHFyeCe_EK60Z4yRUiZrkRknkNAJqYTUk0XPx6Rra2Zpr6omo_quv8Agh6HeTNvS0IMEmgmtc5dhceTm1irZOqmxq705x6SSWoFU0DX9rFu4XlEqWJZ-aSstyfocYpM3yvTG5PgIL4rNEh41my3nZ6rarwrGCsZSSuPCW583zkIF4xkrGD_kSWx-3QoeSeTfuAvPTCuyeGK8-30VHHISOxVxEMYknI9UQrynOhr7LJ7Zp66vtFwf6PkDkP-n7WXgLwAA__9PxYTB) outside their cars. “ _There's a guy with his lawn chairs out. Another lady had sparklers, and her granddaughter was holding the sparkler. And I was like, this is like a party_.” Despite no-parking signs posted weeks earlier, resident Rosa Gomez said people parked everywhere on her street, blocking driveways and access. The standstill blocked emergency vehicles, creating a public safety hazard, as the Dallas County Sheriff’s Office cited overwhelmed resources, and Police Chief Daniel Comeaux pledged future coordination with local officials.

Republicans have controlled the Texas House since 2003 and currently hold an 88-62 majority in the chamber. While the DLCC has not released an official list of races they are targeting, officials are in Corpus Christi, San Benito, Garland, and San Antonio. Democrat Beto O’Rourke, who unsuccessfully ran for the U.S. Senate in 2018, would have carried three of those districts had the current map been in place. One of them—House District 118 in San Antonio— because Republican incumbent John Lujan is running for Congress. Newly appointed House Democratic Campaign Committee Chair Christina Morales said Texas has more Democratic voters than Republicans, and targeted investment and resources can energize them and boost turnout. Fake ICE Officer Arrested Galveston police arrested a 44-year-old man accused of after receiving a complaint from a witness who recorded his alleged attempt to detain two people. Joshua Warner is with two counts of impersonating a public servant and is being held in the Galveston County Jail on a $500,000 bond. Investigators are asking others who may have been unlawfully detained by Warner to come forward. Galveston activists Robert Harvey and Bruce Rebmann approached Warner on Nov. 9, questioning his legitimacy, the uniform looked cheaply made and suspicious. Warner initially refused to provide his name or badge number, then was arrested on Dec. 22 after authorities reported finding a fake badge, false ID, and a vehicle resembling an unmarked patrol car. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---
Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A Tesla Explosion That Changes Everything

Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions.

The details are _shocking_. Gas canisters. Fireworks. A planned detonation.

But here’s the twist...
| [ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=80ffa5ada54d42d0af166510d7604eea&_e=r_EwTqIjftdkto3r4Id617nNAq6-hQ00EERf-sUjfUcsNqaGU2rhMuWv2Cdmq9RlVKBsZlLny6WK78ury8wxh4zb3QFYPmuLU6WZZL9cfpOoyRJxXJ7JX8tsWNvNfxRr7sioJJ9jNfjwDHbGCSyooYDfPdw9Bv33HRQU1fJs6XFNQhPYpWAhz3frfbPcyzn41V5q0jynDusVvcSryZAW-TP9A39v4IQ6JIDRhC5kOXGJKpuIFzCXUoy0Bml8XyPf39jf1krWh1xUQ85WRrDS36g0RhWfoaOQIeLxBs6WEyfmfRBog87gFab-atQgAzqK7AOCB6VduZiD97DFyT4eZUuQE5sKSLS3k9aJ1SuGzLa1VAG2O5bxUs6JX6yhgPu1XeONM8_Y2zr4tgioEvTJZEMvZcWNLHbhKqvhqq7P-Uvfntf3q86D00621KI5gavfBEShn5euJoCbZHv5hYMuL3I5mJe0UGlaRuQzNNUyadeSXxM8qunBs6sg_FH6dAKLinHSs6wR-gCbOlE7Q71XTCp2oy9DZt-KPx9RkW7T4NLa31pVJkfj7dwGXq9j2Z7TJHjO5wMphPv5uznla8RGBw%3D%3D)
---
There’s something bigger behind this story—something most people haven’t noticed.

Do you see it?

[CLICK HERE to uncover the truth.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=80ffa5ada54d42d0af166510d7604eea&_e=r_EwTqIjftdkto3r4Id617nNAq6-hQ00EERf-sUjfUcsNqaGU2rhMuWv2Cdmq9RlVKBsZlLny6WK78ury8wxh4zb3QFYPmuLU6WZZL9cfpOoyRJxXJ7JX8tsWNvNfxRr7sioJJ9jNfjwDHbGCSyooYDfPdw9Bv33HRQU1fJs6XFNQhPYpWAhz3frfbPcyzn4c7n_8ld5pDSiiFXTIVOENljKdRXSbO2r3TrJvqGFa9XAiRKWNM5Dcq63cBHEfyE5LWA7qxGU9Zwy8S5pCNKSnI7JpabmIStuJN4HlEL7BXKFxS5zszCtfgRQYzhqPNaiG86ogjxKqX7wPYsy9qgkmX6FqJd29UHGJ_IyEwm9S_spUgOSXtLmuQ8HeY5RMrvSqSkxXhTwT6Rpee3l76Ng9pfFB_Mj922OLZjXjli5_cuN3WH4vKg1GBc-JljRUkCf_M7SuYghq-24b00_J12j9RgLYgJbLLhyrMLq1aKmKu23ZvQr4ZpvSifI-OwP1VUAzVwlsOyCx8p_fMANTiEneIRnY97vhxmIa-kLwTVL0F2dwP7MZwN0RtPjlYKKIvXEiK6qHHiC9YTD-DaUO9-Oog%3D%3D)

You won’t believe what’s really going on.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.
---

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
